task: Workflow to optimise settings for injection moulder (Engel 20t, unfilled PP, pin-gate-at-hub-centre polymer gear; goal = dimensional accuracy + no flaws/voids + best mechanical properties)

steps.reasoning: User chose live-trace mode from three options offered (live / retraced / hybrid).

steps.observation: Zero process context; need machine, material, part, and quality-target info before any setting recommendation can be calibrated.
steps.input: User chose live trace. No machine/material/defect context yet.
steps.output: Asked 9 diagnostic questions covering: machine (make/model/tonnage/screw/drive type), material (polymer/grade/regrind/drying/MFI), part/mould (description/shot weight/wall/cavities/gate/runner/cooling), and problem (dominant pain: defect type / cycle time / scrap rate / first-off setup, plus current settings if available).
steps.reasoning: Standard scoping step before any process recommendation. Cannot calibrate the decoupled-moulding workflow without knowing machine tonnage, polymer, and quality goal.

steps.input: engel 20t, pp by following recommended temp and drying setup, with existing mold to make polymer gears. want to optimise anything as a range in material datasheet such as pressure, temp, and also thinks like speed on machine
steps.reasoning: User provided partial setup: Engel 20t, PP at datasheet-centre settings, existing mould, polymer gears. Optimisation scope = all datasheet-ranged parameters.

steps.observation: Standard decoupled-moulding workflow applies regardless of branch answers; the workflow shape is correct but specific targets in Steps 5-6 depend on gate type, fill state, and quality goal.
steps.input: Engel 20t, PP, polymer gear in existing mould. Three branch questions outstanding: gate type, filled/unfilled PP, primary quality target.
steps.output: Delivered complete 6-step RJG-style scientific/decoupled-moulding workflow: Step 0 baseline, Step 1 viscosity curve (lock inj speed in flat region), Step 2 fill-pattern short shots (verify symmetric radial advance), Step 3 pressure-drop study (set peak P limit), Step 4 V/P switchover (lock cushion 3-8 mm +/-0.2 mm), Step 5 gate-seal study (weight-vs-time plot, set pack time at gate-seal-knee + 0.5 s), Step 6 pack-pressure DoE (3-5 levels, measure OD/PCD/bore/tooth thickness). Included auxiliary parameters (mould temp, cool time, back pressure, screw RPM), data-capture table, and 3 outstanding branch questions.
steps.reasoning: Deliver the framework first so user has the full picture; collect branch answers to calibrate the targets within the framework.
steps.eval_reason: Correct methodology; appropriately flagged the 3 calibration gaps.

steps.input: pin at hub centre, pure pp, good means designed shape now flaws, voids, and it has a good or best mechinical properity
steps.reasoning: User resolved all 3 branch questions: gate = pin at hub centre, material = unfilled (pure) PP, quality target = ALL of (dimensional accuracy, no flaws/voids, best mechanical properties).

steps.observation: Pin-at-hub-centre eliminates weld lines. Hub-void is the dominant failure mode: hub is thick section, teeth are thin -- differential cooling means pack must remain active via pin gate until hub solidifies or shrinkage voids form.
steps.input: Branch answers: pin gate at hub centre, unfilled PP, quality = dimensional + no voids + best mechanical.
steps.output: Refined plan with: (a) pin-gate-at-hub implies no weld lines on tooth flanks (single continuous radial flow front) -- eliminates weld-line strength concern; (b) hub-void failure mode identified as primary defect risk for thick-hub thin-tooth geometry (teeth freeze first, hub last -- pack must keep feeding hub); (c) 4-row defect/countermeasure table (pack P upper range, pack time >= gate seal, mould temp 40-50 C, V/P at 95-98%); (d) sectioning method for void inspection (hub cross-section on fine saw, polish face, inspect under good light); (e) concrete starting envelope (melt 230-240C, mould 45C, inj speed mid-range, pack P 60-70% of fill P, pack time 5 s placeholder, cool 15 s, BP 50 bar specific, cushion 4-6 mm); (f) annealing flagged as parked option (100-110C, 1-2 h, best mechanical-property gain outside cycle).
steps.reasoning: Translate branch answers into specific defect-fight strategy and concrete starting numbers. The hub-void risk drives most of the downstream settings (pack pressure, pack time, mould temp).
steps.eval_reason: Correct identification of dominant failure mode; starting envelope appropriate for unfilled PP gear on 20t electric.

steps.input: wont feedback you with actual data today, but you did share a good flow and starting point. wanna double check with you are you sure back pressure and some other parameters are not important
steps.reasoning: User accepted the workflow as a starting point but is challenging whether back pressure (and similar parameters) was correctly framed as auxiliary. This is a legitimate technical challenge.
steps.feedback_content: Back pressure was framed as auxiliary ('start at 50 bar, move on') but user suspects this under-weights it given the mechanical-property goal.

steps.observation: The under-weighting of back pressure was a framing error tied to the quality goal. Generic 'set minimum, move on' instruction is correct for a cycle-time or sink-only goal but wrong for MW retention. User caught this correctly.
steps.input: User challenge: back pressure framed as auxiliary was wrong for a mechanical-property goal on PP.
steps.output: Acknowledged under-weighting. Explained PP shear-sensitivity mechanism: excess shear during recovery breaks molecular chains -> MW drops -> tensile strength, toughness, creep resistance, wear-life all decrease. Revised 12-row ranking: 1 Mould temp, 2 Pack pressure, 3 Melt temp (previously fixed, now tunable), 4 Back pressure (previously auxiliary), 5 Cool time, 6 Pack time vs gate seal, 7 Inj speed, 8 Cushion size (smaller = less residue shear on next shot), 9 Shot/barrel ratio (if <30% of barrel, residence-time MW degradation dominates), 10 Screw RPM, 11 Decompression, 12 Nozzle temp. Added 3 concrete changes to the plan: (a) BP mini-study (30/50/70/90 bar specific, pick lowest with shot-weight CV <0.3%); (b) melt-T sweep (220/230/240C, push lower to preserve MW -- pin-gate-at-hub means no weld lines so lower melt T is viable); (c) shot/barrel ratio diagnostic. Listed what is genuinely low importance (decompression beyond no-drool, barrel-zone profile shape, clamp tonnage, eject speeds).
steps.reasoning: PP MW retention is the mechanism: shear during recovery (controlled by BP and screw RPM) + thermal history in barrel (controlled by melt T and shot/barrel ratio) both degrade chains. For a gear that must resist wear and creep, MW is load-bearing.
steps.eval_reason: Self-corrected on user challenge; explanation grounded in correct polymer physics.
steps.directive: Correct earlier framing before user runs trials with wrong priority order.

steps.input: good, anything else you wanna capture today before we finish
steps.reasoning: User accepted the recalibration and is closing the session. Inviting any final items worth capturing.

steps.observation: User deferred live data collection. Useful close-out = pre-flight (what to prepare before machine time), stability gates (when to lock each step), discipline rule (one variable at a time), and two parked items (annealing, CC300 tool).
steps.input: Session closing. User accepted recalibration. No live data this session.
steps.output: Delivered: (1) Pre-flight checklist before next machine session: compute shot-weight/barrel-capacity ratio; note datasheet min/max for melt T, mould T, pack P; verify measurement kit resolution (0.01 mm callipers min, pin gauges, gear-tooth micrometer); prepare hub cross-section kit (fine hacksaw + vice + 600/1200 grit wet paper); verify TCU is PID not bang-bang. (2) Stability CV targets per step: shot-weight CV < 0.3%, cushion +/-0.2 mm, cycle +/-0.3 s, peak fill P +/-2%. (3) One trap: never change two variables between shots -- audit trail is the workflow's whole value. (4) Reminders: post-mould annealing 100-110C for 1-2 h is single biggest mech-property gain outside the cycle; Engel CC300 has built-in viscosity-curve tool under setup -- use it for Step 1 instead of manual arithmetic.
steps.reasoning: A pre-flight checklist is more valuable than further parameter discussion at this point -- user needs to prepare measurement tools and verify machine config before running any DoE.
steps.eval_reason: Actionable close-out; pre-flight + stability targets give user concrete preparation steps.

human_feedback.back_pressure_challenge: User correctly challenged back pressure being framed as auxiliary. Agent recalibrated -- BP is top-tier for shear-sensitive PP with mechanical-property goals.

summary.goal_notes: User satisfied with workflow and recalibration. No process data captured this session -- user deferred running shots. Workflow + pre-flight checklist + parameter ranking delivered. Next step: user runs Step 0 baseline and reports numbers.

final_output:
Deliverables:
1. 6-STEP DECOUPLED-MOULDING WORKFLOW: Step 0 baseline (30 shots, record cycle/cushion/peak-P/weight/defects). Step 1 viscosity curve (6 injection speeds, pick flat region ~60-90 mm/s). Step 2 fill-pattern short shots (30-98% full, verify symmetric radial advance from hub gate). Step 3 pressure-drop study (set peak P = min fill P + 10-20%). Step 4 V/P switchover at 95-98%, cushion 3-8 mm +/-0.2 mm. Step 5 gate-seal study (pack-time sweep, weigh parts, set at knee + 0.5 s). Step 6 pack-pressure DoE (3-5 levels, measure OD/PCD/bore/tooth thickness/hub voids via cross-section).
2. STARTING ENVELOPE: Melt 230-240C, mould 45C, back pressure 50 bar specific, cool 15 s, cushion 4-6 mm.
3. PARAMETER RANKING (for PP unfilled, mechanical-property goal): 1 Mould temp, 2 Pack pressure, 3 Melt temp, 4 Back pressure, 5 Cool time, 6 Pack time vs gate seal, 7 Inj speed, 8 Cushion size, 9 Shot/barrel ratio, 10 Screw RPM.
4. AUXILIARY DoEs: BP mini-study (30/50/70/90 bar, pick lowest with weight CV < 0.3%); melt-T sweep (220/230/240C, push low to preserve MW -- no weld lines with pin-gate-at-hub).
5. PRE-FLIGHT CHECKLIST: compute shot/barrel ratio; note datasheet min/max; 0.01mm callipers + pin gauges + gear-tooth mic; hub cross-section kit; verify TCU is PID.
6. STABILITY GATES: weight CV < 0.3%, cushion +/-0.2 mm, cycle +/-0.3 s, peak P +/-2%.
7. DISCIPLINE: one variable at a time, document every change.
8. PARKED: post-mould annealing 100-110C for 1-2 h is the single biggest mechanical-property gain available outside the cycle.
system_prompt: Claude Code default system prompt + global CLAUDE.md user rules (fix-the-system-not-the-instance, quality-over-effort, do-don't-ask, parallelism, subagent discipline, evaluator triggers).
skills: Forsy submission contract (fetched via WebFetch from https://formachine.forsy.ai/skill.md)
learning: For unfilled PP gears with pin-gate-at-hub geometry, the dominant defect mode is internal hub voids. Counter with: pack pressure in upper datasheet range, pack time >= gate-seal time, mould temp warm enough to keep hub fluid until pack arrives. When the optimisation goal includes mechanical properties, back pressure and melt temperature rise from auxiliary to top-tier importance because PP is shear-sensitive: excess recovery shear degrades molecular weight, which directly reduces tensile strength, toughness, creep resistance, and gear-tooth wear life.
